excerpt.rendered: The Amiga's last hurrah was a console. The chip that ran it deserves a closer look.

content.rendered:
The CD32 shipped in 1993, a 32-bit AGA console with a custom chip called Akiko that did chunky-to-planar conversion in hardware — handy for 3D games on a bitplane machine.

It sold well enough to embarrass the people who thought Commodore was already dead, then Commodore died anyway.
